sn22.79:1.2: “Monks, any contemplatives or brahmans who recollect their manifold past lives all recollect the five clinging-aggregates, or one among them.
sn22.79:1.4: When recollecting, ‘I was one with such a form in the past,’
sn22.79:1.6: Or when recollecting, ‘I was one with such a feeling in the past,’
sn22.79:1.8: Or when recollecting, ‘I was one with such a perception in the past,’ one is recollecting just perception.
sn22.79:1.9: Or when recollecting, ‘I was one with such fabrications in the past,’ one is recollecting just fabrications.
sn22.79:1.10: Or when recollecting, ‘I was one with such a consciousness in the past,’
sn22.79:2.1: And why do you call it ‘form’ [rūpa]?
sn22.79:2.2: ‘It is afflicted [ruppati],’ thus it is called ‘form.’
sn22.79:2.4: With cold & heat & hunger & thirst, with the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, & reptiles.
sn22.79:2.5: ‘It is afflicted,’ thus it is called ‘form.’
sn22.79:3.1: And why do you call it ‘feeling’?
sn22.79:3.4: It feels pleasure, it feels pain, it feels neither-pleasure-nor-pain.
sn22.79:4.1: And why do you call it ‘perception’?
sn22.79:4.4: It perceives blue, it perceives yellow, it perceives red, & it perceives white.
sn22.79:5.1: And why do you call them ‘fabrications’?
sn22.79:5.2: ‘They fabricate the fabricated,’ thus they are called ‘fabrications.’
sn22.79:5.3: And what is the fabricated that they fabricate?
sn22.79:5.4: For the sake of form-ness, they fabricate fabricated form. For the sake of feeling-ness, they fabricate fabricated feeling. For the sake of perception-hood… For the sake of fabrication-hood… For the sake of consciousness-hood, they fabricate fabricated consciousness.
sn22.79:5.5: ‘They fabricate the fabricated,’ thus they are called ‘fabrications.’
sn22.79:6.1: And why do you call it ‘consciousness’?
sn22.79:6.4: It cognizes sour, it cognizes bitter, it cognizes pungent, it cognizes sweet, it cognizes alkaline, it cognizes non-alkaline, it cognizes salty, & it cognizes unsalty.
sn22.79:7.1: Thus an instructed disciple of the noble ones reflects in this way:
sn22.79:7.2: ‘I am now being chewed up by form.
sn22.79:7.3: But in the past I was also chewed up by form in the same way I am now being chewed up by present form.
sn22.79:7.4: And if I delight in future form, then in the future I will be chewed up by form in the same way I am now being chewed up by present form.’
sn22.79:7.5: Having reflected in this way, he becomes indifferent to past form,
sn22.79:7.6: does not delight in future form,
sn22.79:7.7: and is practicing for the sake of disenchantment, dispassion, and cessation with regard to present form.
sn22.79:8.1: (He reflects:) ‘I am now being chewed up by feeling…
sn22.79:10.2: But in the past I was also chewed up by consciousness in the same way I am now being chewed up by present consciousness.
sn22.79:10.3: And if I delight in future consciousness, then in the future I will be chewed up by consciousness in the same way I am now being chewed up by present consciousness.’
sn22.79:10.5: Having reflected in this way, he becomes indifferent to past consciousness,
sn22.79:10.6: does not delight in future consciousness,
sn22.79:10.7: and is practicing for the sake of disenchantment, dispassion, and cessation with regard to present consciousness.
sn22.79:11.4: “And is that which is inconstant easeful or stressful?”
sn22.79:11.6: “And is it fitting to regard what is inconstant, stressful, subject to change as:
sn22.79:11.7: ‘This is mine. This is my self. This is what I am’?”
sn22.79:11.9: “… Is feeling constant or inconstant?”—“Inconstant, lord.” …
sn22.79:11.10: “… Is perception constant or inconstant?”—“Inconstant, lord.” …
sn22.79:11.11: “… Are fabrications constant or inconstant?”—“Inconstant, lord.” …
sn22.79:11.12: “What do you think, monks? Is consciousness constant or inconstant?”
sn22.79:11.14: “And is that which is inconstant easeful or stressful?”
sn22.79:11.16: “And is it fitting to regard what is inconstant, stressful, subject to change as:
sn22.79:11.17: ‘This is mine. This is my self. This is what I am’?”
sn22.79:11.19: “Thus, monks, any form whatsoever that is past, future, or present; internal or external; blatant or subtle; common or sublime; far or near: Every form is to be seen as it has come to be with right discernment as: ‘This is not mine. This is not my self. This is not what I am.’
sn22.79:11.23: Any consciousness whatsoever that is past, future, or present; internal or external; blatant or subtle; common or sublime; far or near: Every consciousness is to be seen as it has come to be with right discernment as: ‘This is not mine. This is not my self. This is not what I am.’
sn22.79:12.1: This, monks, is called a disciple of the noble ones who tears down and does not build up;
sn22.79:12.2: who abandons and does not cling;
sn22.79:12.3: who discards and does not pull in;
sn22.79:12.4: who scatters and does not pile up.
sn22.79:12.5: And what does he tear down and not build up?
sn22.79:12.6: He tears down form and does not build it up.
sn22.79:12.10: consciousness and does not build it up.
sn22.79:12.11: And what does he abandon and not cling to?
sn22.79:12.12: He abandons form and does not cling to it.
sn22.79:12.16: consciousness and does not cling to it.
sn22.79:12.17: And what does he discard and not pull in?
sn22.79:12.18: He discards form and does not pull it in.
sn22.79:12.22: consciousness and does not pull it in.
sn22.79:12.23: And what does he scatter and not pile up?
sn22.79:12.24: He scatters form and does not pile it up.
sn22.79:12.28: consciousness and does not pile it up.
sn22.79:13.1: Seeing thus, the instructed disciple of the noble ones grows disenchanted with form, disenchanted with feeling, disenchanted with perception, disenchanted with fabrications, disenchanted with consciousness.
sn22.79:13.2: Disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion, he is released. With release, there is the knowledge, ‘Released.’
sn22.79:13.3: He discerns that ‘Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done. There is nothing further for this world.’
sn22.79:14.1: This, monks, is called a disciple of the noble ones who neither builds up nor tears down, but who stands having torn down; who neither clings nor abandons, but who stands having abandoned; who neither pulls in nor discards, but who stands having discarded; who neither piles up nor scatters, but who stands having scattered.
sn22.79:14.2: And what is it that he neither builds up nor tears down, but stands having torn it down?
sn22.79:14.3: He neither builds up nor tears down form, but stands having torn it down.
sn22.79:14.4: He neither builds up nor tears down feeling…
sn22.79:14.7: consciousness, but stands having torn it down.
sn22.79:14.8: And what is it that he neither clings to nor abandons, but stands having abandoned it?
sn22.79:14.9: He neither clings to nor abandons form, but stands having abandoned it.
sn22.79:14.10: He neither clings to nor abandons feeling…
sn22.79:14.13: consciousness, but stands having abandoned it.
sn22.79:14.14: And what is it that he neither pulls in nor discards, but stands having discarded it?
sn22.79:14.15: He neither pulls in nor discards form, but stands having discarded it.
sn22.79:14.16: He neither pulls in nor discards feeling…
sn22.79:14.19: consciousness, but stands having discarded it.
sn22.79:14.20: And what is it that he neither piles up nor scatters, but stands having scattered it?
sn22.79:14.21: He neither piles up nor scatters form, but stands having scattered it.
sn22.79:14.22: He neither piles up nor scatters feeling…
sn22.79:14.25: consciousness, but stands having scattered it.
sn22.79:14.26: And to the monk whose mind is thus released, the devas, together with Indra, the Brahmās, & Pajāpatis, pay homage even from afar:
sn22.79:15.2: Homage to you, O superlative man—
sn22.79:15.3: you of whom we don’t know even what
